title: After-Sale Service Training Manual Excerpt
title_zh: 售後服務培訓手冊摘錄

all.en: This chapter introduces the foundations of effective after-sale service.
all.zh: 本章介紹有效售後服務的基礎原則。

all.en: After-sale service starts the moment the customer signs the contract.
all.zh: 售後服務自客戶簽訂合約那一刻起便已開始。

all.en: It continues throughout the entire product lifecycle, often for many years.
all.zh: 並持續整個產品生命週期，通常長達數年。

all.en: Loyal customers generate more revenue than newly acquired ones do.
all.zh: 忠誠客戶帶來的收益比新獲取的客戶更多。

all.en: A satisfied customer also refers others through word of mouth.
all.zh: 滿意的客戶亦會以口碑介紹他人。

all.en: Each service interaction should follow the same four-step framework.
all.zh: 每次服務互動應遵循相同的四步驟架構。

all.en: Step one, greet the customer with warmth and confirm their request.
all.zh: 步驟一，熱情問候客戶並確認其需求。

all.en: Use the customer name at least twice during the conversation.
all.zh: 對話中至少使用客戶姓名兩次。

all.en: Step two, investigate the issue thoroughly before suggesting a solution.
all.zh: 步驟二，在提出解決方案前徹底了解問題。

all.en: Ask open questions that begin with what, when, or how.
all.zh: 提出以「什麼」、「何時」或「如何」起頭的開放式問題。

all.en: Step three, present a solution and explain why it fits the situation.
all.zh: 步驟三，提出方案並說明為何適合該情況。

all.en: Confirm the customer agreement before scheduling any field service.
all.zh: 安排任何現場服務前須確認客戶同意。

all.en: Step four, follow up within seventy-two hours to verify satisfaction.
all.zh: 步驟四，於 72 小時內追蹤以確認客戶滿意度。

all.en: Document the entire interaction in the service log promptly.
all.zh: 請及時於服務日誌中記錄整個互動過程。

all.en: Handling difficult conversations requires both empathy and professional boundaries.
all.zh: 處理棘手對話須兼具同理心與專業分寸。

all.en: Acknowledge the customer feelings without admitting fault prematurely.
all.zh: 肯認客戶感受，但勿過早承認責任歸屬。

all.en: Use phrases such as I understand this has been frustrating for you.
all.zh: 可使用如「我了解這對您而言令人挫折」等語句。

all.en: Offer concrete next steps rather than vague promises of follow-up.
all.zh: 提供具體後續步驟，而非含糊的追蹤承諾。

all.en: Escalate to a senior agent when the request falls outside policy.
all.zh: 若需求超出政策範圍，請升級至資深專員處理。

all.en: Never argue with customers, even when their statements appear inaccurate.
all.zh: 即便客戶陳述似有不實，也不得與其爭辯。

all.en: Protect customer data by verifying identity before sharing account details.
all.zh: 分享帳戶資訊前先驗證身份，以保護客戶資料。

all.en: Record any commitments you make and ensure your team can honor them.
all.zh: 記錄您所做之承諾，並確保團隊能夠兌現。

all.en: Continuous learning is essential because products and policies change frequently.
all.zh: 持續學習至關重要，因產品與政策時常變動。

all.en: Complete monthly refresher modules to maintain your service certification.
all.zh: 請完成每月複訓模組以維持服務認證資格。

all.en: Peer shadowing each quarter exposes you to alternative resolution techniques.
all.zh: 每季同儕跟隨見習可讓您接觸不同的處理技巧。

all.en: Service supervisors hold weekly calibration sessions every Thursday afternoon.
all.zh: 服務主管於每週四下午舉行校準會議。

all.en: Use the knowledge base before asking colleagues to save shared time.
all.zh: 詢問同事前請先查閱知識庫，以節省彼此時間。

all.en: Submit suggested articles through the content improvement form on the portal.
all.zh: 可透過入口網站的內容改善表單提交建議文章。

all.en: Customer satisfaction scores feed directly into your annual performance review.
all.zh: 客戶滿意度分數將直接納入您的年度績效考核。

all.en: High performers may qualify for the quarterly service excellence award.
all.zh: 績效優異者可獲提名季度服務卓越獎。

all.en: Award recipients receive a bonus and recognition at the all-hands meeting.
all.zh: 得獎者將獲得獎金並於全體員工大會中受表揚。

all.en: Remember that every call shapes our brand reputation in the marketplace.
all.zh: 請謹記每通電話皆形塑我們於市場上的品牌聲譽。

all.en: Thank you for the care and pride you bring to your work daily.
all.zh: 感謝您每日以用心與自豪投入工作。

all.en: Please proceed to the practice exercises located at the end of this chapter.
all.zh: 請接續完成本章末的實作練習。

all.en: Exercise one presents a sample call requiring application of all four steps.
all.zh: 練習一提供一通示範通話，須運用所有四個步驟回應。

all.en: Review the transcript carefully before drafting your response notes.
all.zh: 請仔細審閱通話紀錄再撰寫您的回應筆記。

all.en: Exercise two pairs you with a colleague for live role-play practice.
all.zh: 練習二安排您與同事配對進行現場角色扮演演練。

all.en: Rotate roles every fifteen minutes to experience both customer and agent perspectives.
all.zh: 每 15 分鐘輪換角色，以體會客戶與專員兩種視角。

all.en: Exercise three asks you to analyze a recorded escalation case from last year.
all.zh: 練習三請您分析一段去年錄製的升級處理案例。

all.en: Identify three things the agent did well and two areas for improvement.
all.zh: 請指出該專員做得好的三件事及兩項可改進之處。

all.en: Submit your written analysis through the training portal before next Monday.
all.zh: 請於下週一前透過訓練平台繳交您的書面分析。

all.en: Your facilitator will provide written feedback within five business days.
all.zh: 您的引導者將於 5 個工作天內提供書面回饋。

all.en: Group debrief sessions are scheduled for the following Friday afternoon.
all.zh: 團體檢討會議排定於隔週週五下午舉行。

all.en: Welcome questions during the debrief and bring concrete examples to discuss.
all.zh: 歡迎於檢討時提問，並請攜帶具體案例供討論。

all.en: Honestly, my mother once stayed on hold for forty minutes just to thank a service agent.
all.zh: 老實說，我媽曾為了感謝一位客服人員，在線上等了四十分鐘。

all.en: She wanted the supervisor to know that the agent saved her week with one kind phone call.
all.zh: 她想讓主管知道，那位專員用一通溫暖的電話救了她整個禮拜。

all.en: Stories like hers remind me that small gestures travel further than we ever imagine.
all.zh: 她的故事提醒我，小小的舉動傳得比我們想像的遠很多。

all.en: Please try writing one customer name on a sticky note today and remember why they called.
all.zh: 請試著在今天找一張便利貼，寫下一位客戶的名字，並記得他來電的原因。

all.en: Tomorrow when you see that note, your follow up tone will sound completely different.
all.zh: 明天當你看到那張便利貼，你後續追蹤的語氣會完全不一樣。

all.en: Last week I forgot a customer name and felt embarrassed during the callback I had promised.
all.zh: 上週我答應好的回電電話中，因為忘了客戶名字而尷尬到不行。

all.en: She graciously laughed it off, but I promised myself to never let that happen again.
all.zh: 她大方地笑笑帶過，但我跟自己約定，再也不要讓這種事發生。

all.en: Try drinking water between calls to keep your voice warm and your patience steady.
all.zh: 通話間請喝點水，可以讓嗓音保持溫潤，耐性也比較穩。

all.en: A colleague keeps a small plant on her desk and says it reminds her to breathe.
all.zh: 有位同事在桌上養了一盆小植物，她說那盆植物提醒她要好好呼吸。

all.en: Tiny rituals like that often make a long shift feel a little kinder to yourself.
all.zh: 這種小儀式，常常能讓漫長的班次對自己溫柔一點。
